steps.title: Use Rafael's stated motive
steps.content: The passage says Rafael hoped not only to meet the Ureña girls but also to make the acquaintance of their father, the poet Nicolás Ureña.

steps.title: Reject added motives
steps.content: A transfers the narrator's learning situation to Rafael, B adds a request for mentorship, and C invents a plan to present poems. The sentence gives Rafael's two goals explicitly, so no broader ambition has to be inferred. The stated motive is meeting both the sisters and their father. The answer is D.
